text: The surgical field is prepared with the patient in lithotomy position, using a lone star retractor for exposure. Saline and adrenaline solution is injected along the posterior vaginal wall and rectocele to minimize bleeding and facilitate dissection. A transverse vaginal mucosal incision is made above the introitus, and the rectovaginal space is dissected to separate the rectocele from the posterior vaginal wall. Blunt dissection is performed through an avascular space up to the pouch of Douglas and laterally to the pubertalis muscle. Midline longitudinal plication of the rectal wall is performed using absorbable sutures, focusing on the muscular layer without entering the rectal lumen, extending distally to the anal-rectal junction.
